rulings.Case Title: THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA VS. COUGHLIN, MARY S. (STATE BAR NO.104129)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Order For Disposition Of Unclaimed Client Files
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday February 24, 2012 line 1 - PLAINTIFF THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Order For Disposition Of Unclaimed Client Files. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.[The motion is unopposed. We have not received the moving courtesy copies.]=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MALCOLM MEYERS VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 24, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA MERCY MEDICAL CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first cause of action because there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the ramp constituted a dangerous condition and whether it was an open and obvious condition of the property. While the Court does not believe that the doctrine of negligence per se applies to this case, the Court cannot summarily adjudicate that issue under CCP 437c(f)(1). Defendant may raise that issue by a motion in limine. The Court grants summary adjudication on the second cause of action. Plaintiff was not denied full and equal access as a matter of law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SPRUELL et al. VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 24, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., FOR ITSELF AND AS SUCCESSOR DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the first, second and fourth causes of action, sustained, without leave to amend, as to the third cause of action and sustained, with 10 days leave to amend the sixth cause of action. Plaintiffs have pled sufficient facts addressing the statute of limitations and to allege intentional and negligent misrepresentation and aiding and abetting. As to the third cause of action, the relationship between defendant Bank of America, a lending institution, and plaintiffs, as borrower-clients, is not fiduciary in nature. As to the sixth cause of action, leave to amend is granted if plaintiffs can allege in good faith that that defendant Bank of America not only intended to perform the acts which caused the interference, but that it also intended to cause the result itself. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: THE PAYROLL RESOURCE GROUP, A CALIFORNIA VS. ELIZABETH ECKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 24, 2012, Line 20, DEFENDANT ELIZABETH ECKE, PAYROLL SYSTEMS LLC Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Motion For Sanctions - The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, as follows: Granted, in part, subject to the parties' protective order. Within five calendar days of the hearing, Defendants shall propose any changes to the proposed protective order to Plaintiff. If the parties come to an agreement on the form of the protective order within five calendar days after that point, Plaintiff shall submit the stipulated protective order to the court for signature and entry without hearing. If the parties cannot agree on the form of the protective order within that time, the parties are required to notify the court accordingly. The court adds that the parties shall each submit their proposed protective order, and the court shall issue a protective order in the form determined by the court. Within 20 calendar days from the entry of the protective order, Plaintiff shall serve further responses, without objection but subject to the protective order, to Defendants' Form Interrogatories Nos. 8.7, 9.1, 9.2, 17.1, and 50.1; Defendants' Special Interrogatories Nos. 13, 15, 16, 17, 22, 23, 25, 26, 36, and 37; and Defendants' Requests for Production Nos. 4, 5, 9, 28, 29, 32, 34, 41, 42, 44, 45, and 46, and shall produce all documents responsive to those requests. Plaintiff contended at hearing that it has no further information to include in its further response to any of these interrogatories or requests, and that it has given all of the information it has. If that is so, Plaintiff shall explain what efforts were made to obtain the information and state all facts - not just contentions - regarding why a more detailed response or more information or documents cannot be given. Any documents produced by Plaintiff shall be produced in the manner in which they were kept, or organized and labeled to correspond with the categories in Defendants' request. See Code Civ. Proc. § 2031.280(a). If the documents are produced in a disorganized state due to the fact that Plaintiff kept the documents in such a state, Plaintiff shall indicate that accordingly in its response, with facts stated upon personal knowledge. See Kayne v. Grande Holdings Ltd., 198 Cal.App.4th 1470, 1475-76 (2011). Plaintiff is not required to provide further responses to Defendants' Special Interrogatories Nos. 33 and 34 or to Defendants' Requests for Production Nos. 37 and 38. Monetary sanctions of $ 2,000.00 are issued against Plaintiff. The court adds a due date of March 30, 2012 for payment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SRB)
